name: Minestrone
recipeIngredient: 1/4 pound pancetta, or sliced lean bacon (chopped)
1/4 pound green beans, trimmed and cut into 1/2-inch pieces
1/2 pound kale, leaves rinsed and drained
recipeInstructions:
1. In a large bowl, let the white beans soak in enough water to cover them by 2 inches overnight or quick-soak them. Drain the beans.
2. In a heavy kettle, cook the pancetta in olive oil over moderate heat until crisp and pale golden. Add onion and cook until softened.
3. Add carrots, celery, and garlic; stir for 4 minutes. Add zucchini, green beans, and potatoes (peeled and cut into 3/4-inch dice); stir for 4 minutes.
4. Add cabbage and kale; stir until cabbage is wilted. Stir in tomatoes and broth; simmer the soup, covered, for 1 hour.
5. In a blender or food processor, puree half of the beans with 1 cup reserved liquid. Stir this puree and remaining beans into the soup.
6. Simmer the soup uncovered for 15 minutes, thinning with more reserve liquid if desired. Season with salt and pepper.
7. The soup can be made up to 3 days in advance and kept covered and chilled. Reheat before serving.
8. Serve hot with Parmesan, bruschetta, and sausages.
Makes about 10 cups, serving 6-8.
